author: lOrm
content: Verified at HEAD 80439851: the headline defect (fork+exec of  per 50ms tick) is OBSOLETE. src/tools/bash/kill.rs:60 now uses  directly (zero subprocesses, async-signal-safe). The remaining surface is a 50ms std::thread::sleep busy-wait, which is bounded by the 2s deadline and amounts to ~40 libc calls — well within acceptable cost for a process-termination hot path. The nix::waitpid/WNOHANG alternative the QA review mandated assumed the per-tick subprocess cost that is no longer present. Closing as partially-addressed; the underlying performance concern is resolved. If the residual 50ms sleep granularity becomes load-bearing (e.g., for high-frequency shell churn), file a fresh ticket focused on the sleep, not the obsolete subprocess complaint.
